runtimes.name: receiptsagent
runtimes.description: Receipts IDP agent — dual-agent extraction with a model degradation ladder (Phase 1: stub)

evaluators.name: ReceiptsExtractionQualityEvaluator
evaluators.description: LLM-as-judge for receipts extraction quality: OCR field accuracy, totals reconciliation, category plausibility, routing correctness, and honest confidence (spec §5.6).

numerical.label: Poor
numerical.definition: Major extraction errors or wrong routing

numerical.label: Average
numerical.definition: Correct extraction but reconciliation or routing could be tighter

numerical.label: Good
numerical.definition: Accurate extraction with correct routing and clear reasoning

numerical.label: Excellent
numerical.definition: Perfect extraction, reconciliation, category, and routing

onlineEvalConfigs.name: ReceiptsEvaluation
onlineEvalConfigs.description: Online evaluation for the receipts agent (3 built-in metrics, scored continuously from production spans).

agentCoreGateways.name: ReceiptsGateway
agentCoreGateways.description: MCP Gateway for receipts tools: user profile, recent expenses, merchant lookup, save expense, human review

policyEngines.name: ReceiptsPolicyEngine
policyEngines.description: Cedar policy engine for receipts authorization — allow-all + selective deny on save-expense over a threshold (routes to human review)

policies.name: AllowAllTools
policies.description: Permit all tool calls on the receipts gateway for authenticated agents

policies.name: BlockExcessiveExpense
policies.description: Forbid SAVING an expense with total >= $2,000; the agent routes it to human_review instead. Guard order matters (Cedar && short-circuits): (1) `context has input` FIRST so the statement is valid for actions like AgentCore::Action::Http that have no input (omitting this fails policy-engine CREATE with 'attribute input not found' — IGNORE_ALL_FINDINGS does NOT suppress it); (2) `!(context.input has reason)` scopes to the save path (human_review requires reason, save_expense has none), so the review fallback is never blocked; (3) the >= 2000 threshold. NOTE: integer totals get exact semantics; a fractional total makes the decimal-vs-Long comparison error and (forbid-fails-closed) routes that save to review too — a safe, conservative 'when in doubt, review' outcome (spec §5.5).
policies.statement: forbid(principal, action, resource is AgentCore::Gateway) when { context has input && !(context.input has reason) && context.input has total && context.input.total >= 2000 };
